sections.paragraph:
- Original: τράχηλος

- Transliteration: Trachelos

- Phonetic: trakh'-ay-los

- Definition:

1. to be ready to incur the most imminent peril to life

- Origin: probably from G5143 (through the idea of mobility)

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: Probably from G5143 (through the idea of mobility); the throat (neck) that is (figuratively) life: - neck.
